lines.text: Letter to Director
June 8, 1954
which [REDACTED] had with a crew of assumed space explorers under
the supervision of a ravishing woman commandant in the Nevada desert.
The article also reflects that [REDACTED] allegedly was aboard
flying saucers on eleven occasions. At the bottom of the first page
of issue number 15 there appeared in what Mr. [REDACTED] stated was the
handwriting of [REDACTED] the statement "This is a true story, a
factual experience -- [REDACTED]
The above mentioned article in "Valor" also reflected that [REDACTED]
[REDACTED] is 55 years of age and that his residence address is [REDACTED]
[REDACTED], Redondo Beach, California.
Mr. [REDACTED] also had with him a letter addressed to [REDACTED]
from [REDACTED] of the Saucers Research Foundation, which letter re-
flected that that organization was holding a three-day convention June 4,
5 and 6, 1954 at the Carthay Circle Theatre in Los Angeles, California,
and that it had invited [REDACTED] to this convention to say a few
words concerning his experience. [REDACTED] was identified in this letter as
business manager of [REDACTED]
[REDACTED] Hollywood 28, California.
Mr. [REDACTED] reiterated that he felt [REDACTED]'s story, if true,
should be given wide publicity, but that if the story was false, then he
should be prosecuted for fraud. He stated that he first took his in-
formation to the Air Force in the person of Lieutenant Colonel [REDACTED]
[REDACTED] of Wright-Patterson Air Force Base, Ohio, whom [REDACTED] visited
personally at [REDACTED] home in Fairborn, Ohio, on Sunday, 6-6-54.
[REDACTED] indicated that he asked Colonel [REDACTED] if there were such things
as flying saucers and if [REDACTED] story could be true, and he said
[REDACTED] denied that there was such a thing as a flying saucer and indicated
to him that DONALD E. KEHOE, the author of "Flying Saucers in Outer
Space" was a fraud and that information is available in Washington that
KEHOE is a fraud. He said he was advised, however, by [REDACTED]
that the Air Force could take no action with respect to [REDACTED]
[REDACTED]
[REDACTED] continued that it was his intention to aid in the
promotion of a meeting for [REDACTED] in Cincinnati. He advised that the
original meeting scheduled for June 11 had been cancelled, and that he
felt this meeting had such important information for the people as a whole
that he was going to make every effort to make the meeting one of national
